summary: CSRF attack successfully demonstrated by having victim browser automatically submit malicious configuration to REST endpoint when visiting attacker-controlled page.
expected_security_impact: When victim visits attacker's malicious HTML page while authenticated to Traefik API, the page should automatically send PUT request with attacker-controlled configuration, and Traefik should accept and apply the malicious routing rules.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh started Traefik with API enabled, served attacker HTML page, simulated victim visit, and observed Traefik configuration change to route traffic to attacker-controlled backend. Verification confirmed malicious frontend/backend were added to active configuration.
verification_method: The PoC sets up: (1) Traefik with REST API, (2) Attacker HTML page with auto-submitting form, (3) Simulated victim browser visit. Verification queries Traefik API to confirm malicious configuration was accepted and applied. Success is confirmed when attacker backend appears in /api response.
evidence_produced_by_run: Traefik started with REST provider enabled on port 8080.
Attacker HTML page created with JavaScript fetch() to PUT /api/providers/rest.
Malicious configuration included backend pointing to attacker server (http://evil.example.com).
Simulated victim visit triggered automatic form submission.
Traefik API response after attack showed new frontend 'attacker-frontend' in configuration.
Backend 'attacker-backend' with URL http://evil.example.com confirmed in active config.
Original configuration was overwritten, demonstrating full control via CSRF.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones Traefik v1.7 and mounts into container.
